title: anthriscus vulgaris var. neglecta
intro: Anthriscus vulgaris var. neglecta, commonly known as Wild Chervil or Queen Anne's Lace, is a biennial plant belonging to the carrot family. It has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is also rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, providing several health benefits such as boosting the immune system and reducing the risk of certain diseases. Anthriscus vulgaris var. neglecta can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, which are all popular herbal preparations for its medicinal properties.
